turn_a: Where do you come from, Socrates? And yet I need hardly ask the question, for I know that you have been in chase of the fair Alcibiades. I saw him the day before yesterday; and he had got a beard like a man,--and he is a man, as I may tell you in your ear. But I thought that he was still very charming.
turn_b: What of his beard? Are you not of Homer's opinion, who says 'Youth is most charming when the beard first appears'? And that is now the charm of Alcibiades.

turn_a: What of his beard? Are you not of Homer's opinion, who says 'Youth is most charming when the beard first appears'? And that is now the charm of Alcibiades.
turn_b: Well, and how do matters proceed? Have you been visiting him, and was he gracious to you?

turn_a: Well, and how do matters proceed? Have you been visiting him, and was he gracious to you?
turn_b: Yes, I thought that he was very gracious; and especially to-day, for I have just come from him, and he has been helping me in an argument. But shall I tell you a strange thing? I paid no attention to him, and several times I quite forgot that he was present.

turn_a: Yes, I thought that he was very gracious; and especially to-day, for I have just come from him, and he has been helping me in an argument. But shall I tell you a strange thing? I paid no attention to him, and several times I quite forgot that he was present.
turn_b: What is the meaning of this? Has anything happened between you and him? For surely you cannot have discovered a fairer love than he is; certainly not in this city of Athens.

turn_a: And is this stranger really in your opinion a fairer love than the son of Cleinias?
turn_b: And is not the wiser always the fairer, sweet friend?

turn_a: And is not the wiser always the fairer, sweet friend?
turn_b: But have you really met, Socrates, with some wise one?

turn_a: But have you really met, Socrates, with some wise one?
turn_b: Say rather, with the wisest of all living men, if you are willing to accord that title to Protagoras.

turn_a: Say rather, with the wisest of all living men, if you are willing to accord that title to Protagoras.

turn_b: Yes; he has been here two days.

turn_a: Yes; he has been here two days.
turn_b: And do you just come from an interview with him?

turn_a: And do you just come from an interview with him?
turn_b: Yes; and I have heard and said many things.

turn_a: Yes; and I have heard and said many things.
turn_b: Then, if you have no engagement, suppose that you sit down and tell me what passed, and my attendant here shall give up his place to you.

turn_a: Then, if you have no engagement, suppose that you sit down and tell me what passed, and my attendant here shall give up his place to you.
turn_b: To be sure; and I shall be grateful to you for listening.

turn_a: To be sure; and I shall be grateful to you for listening.
turn_b: Thank you, too, for telling us.
